info.title: STD Plus API
info.description: Backend API for STD Plus — materials, inventory, orders, and workflow management.

role.description: Role ObjectId (populated with full role object including permissions)

panes.description: Optional array of panes to create alongside the request

currentStation.description: Station ObjectId — null for virtual states (queue/ready/claimed)

jobType.description: What the customer wants done (e.g. Laminated, Tempered, Beveled)

holes.description: Array of hole positions and dimensions

notches.description: Array of notch positions and dimensions

stations.description: Array of Station objects (populated with _id, name)

stationBreakdown.description: Pane count per station — keys are Station ObjectId strings

paneCount.description: Total number of panes in this order
Total number of panes in this order

inventory.description: Inventory slot the material was withdrawn from

defectStation.description: Station where defect was found (populated with _id, name)
Station ObjectId where defect was found

paneNumber.description: Pane number (e.g. PNE-0001). The endpoint auto-resolves the order and material from the pane.

defectStation.description: Station ObjectId where defect was found
Station ObjectId where defect was found

remakeStation.description: Station ObjectId where the remake pane should land (e.g. order release station). Frontend sends this when approving a claim.

colorId.description: Station color identifier for UI display

isLaminateStation.description: If true, this station is where laminate sheet panes are merged into parent panes
Mark as lamination station where sheets merge
Mark as lamination station where sheets merge

name.description: Display name (e.g. หัวหน้าช่าง)

permissions.description: Permission keys (e.g. orders:view, panes:scan). Use '*' for full access.

isSystem.description: System roles (admin, manager, worker) cannot be deleted

panes.description: Array of Pane references associated with this log entry

order.description: Populated when an order is created from the request

material.description: Populated when an order is created from the request (inherited from the order's material)

inventory.description: Inventory slot this pane was cut from (if any)

currentStation.description: Populated Station object (with _id, name) — null for virtual states (queued, completed, claimed)

routing.description: Array of Station objects (populated with _id, name)

jobType.description: What the customer wants done (e.g. Laminated, Tempered, Beveled)

rawGlass.description: Raw material info for warehouse withdrawal

color.description: Glass color (e.g. เขียว, ใส, ชา, เทา)

sheetsPerPane.description: Number of raw sheets needed per pane (2 for Laminated)

holes.description: Array of hole positions and dimensions

notches.description: Array of notch positions and dimensions

processes.description: Processing steps required (e.g. temper, laminate, assembly, cnc)

laminateRole.description: single = normal pane, parent = final laminated product (dormant until merge), sheet = raw glass sheet going through production

parentPane.description: For sheets: the parent pane they belong to

childPanes.description: For parents: list of child sheet panes

sheetLabel.description: Sheet identifier within a laminate group (A, B, etc.)

laminateStation.description: Station where sheets merge into the parent

request.description: Request ID this pane belongs to

order.description: Order ID (optional, auto-filled when order is created from request)

material.description: Material ID (optional, auto-filled from the order's material when order is created from request)

inventory.description: Inventory slot ID. If provided, a 'cut' MaterialLog is auto-created for traceability.

jobType.description: What the customer wants done (e.g. Laminated, Tempered, Beveled)

holes.description: Array of hole positions and dimensions

notches.description: Array of notch positions and dimensions

processes.description: Processing steps required (e.g. temper, laminate, assembly, cnc)

station.description: Station ObjectId where the scan is happening (must match the pane's currentStation)

action.description: `scan_in` — acknowledge arrival, `start` — begin work, `complete` — mark work done (pane stays at station with `awaiting_scan_out` status), `scan_out` — handoff pane to next station (or mark completed if last station), `laminate` — merge child sheets into parent pane (call on parent paneNumber, all active sheets must be at the lamination station)

operator.description: Worker ID. Accepted by validation but currently unused — the authenticated user (`req.user._id`) is always recorded as the worker in the PaneLog.

log.description: PaneLog entry created by the scan

nextStation.description: Station ObjectId the pane moved to (only present on `scan_out` action, null if last station)

mergedSheets.description: Number of child sheets merged (only present on `laminate` action)

jobType.description: What the customer wants done (e.g. Laminated, Tempered, Beveled)

holes.description: Array of hole positions and dimensions

notches.description: Array of notch positions and dimensions

processes.description: Processing steps required (e.g. temper, laminate, assembly, cnc)

name.description: Display name (e.g. ลามิเนต)

sheetsPerPane.description: Number of raw sheets needed per pane (2 for Laminated)

defaultRawGlassTypes.description: Suggested raw glass types (e.g. Clear, Tinted)

elements.description: JSON array of sticker layout elements
JSON array of sticker layout elements
JSON array of sticker layout elements

SortParam.name: sort
SortParam.description: Sort field. Prefix with - for descending (e.g. -createdAt, name)

409.description: Cannot delete — referenced by other records

200.description: Login successful. The `worker` object includes a populated `role` (full role document with `permissions`, not only an id).

get.description: Returns the full list of permission keys that can be assigned to roles. Requires authentication.

delete.description: Requires `roles:manage` permission. System roles cannot be deleted. Roles with assigned workers cannot be deleted.

patch.description: Requires `roles:manage` permission. System role slugs cannot be changed.

delete.description: Requires `roles:manage` permission. System roles cannot be deleted. Roles with assigned workers cannot be deleted.

post.description: Moves a specified quantity from the source inventory record to a target location. If an inventory record already exists at the target location (same material and stock type), the quantity is added to it; otherwise a new record is created. Requires `inventory:move` permission.
Automatically deducts from matching inventory. Returns 400 if insufficient stock.

get.description: Requires `orders:view` permission. Users without `orders:manage` permission see only orders assigned to them.

parameters.name: stationId
parameters.description: Filter orders that include this station in their route

post.description:
Requires `orders:create` permission.

When a `request` ID is provided, all panes linked to that request (that don't already have an order) are automatically updated with the new order's `_id` and `material`.

patch.description: Requires `orders:view` permission. Users without `orders:manage` permission can only update their own assigned orders.

post.summary: Create a claim for an order
post.description:
Requires `claims:create` permission. Creates a new claim linked to the specified order.

**Side effect:** If a `pane` ID is provided and that pane is currently in a station (not yet completed), the pane is **pulled from the station system** — its `currentStation` is set to `null` and `currentStatus` to `claimed`, and the order's `stationBreakdown` is decremented accordingly. The order's `paneCount` is also **decremented** and `progressPercent` is recalculated so that progress tracking remains accurate after the pane is removed. Real-time events are emitted so the frontend removes the pane from station views.

Convenience endpoint that accepts a `paneNumber` (e.g. `PNE-0001`) and automatically resolves the pane's `order` and `material`. No need to provide order ID in the URL or material ID in the body.

Requires `claims:create` permission.

**Side effect:** The claimed pane is **pulled from the station system** — its `currentStation` is set to `null` and `currentStatus` to `claimed`, and the order's `stationBreakdown` is decremented accordingly. The order's `paneCount` is also **decremented** and `progressPercent` is recalculated so that progress tracking remains accurate after the pane is removed. Real-time events are emitted so the frontend removes the pane from station views. Already-completed panes are not affected.

400.description: Pane has no associated order or material

patch.description:
Requires `claims:view` permission. Users without `claims:manage` permission can only update their own claims.

**Approval orchestration:** When `status` is changed to `approved` (from any other status) and no `remadePane` exists yet, the system automatically:

1. Creates a **new remake pane** with the same specs as the original (dimensions, rawGlass, jobType, glassType, processes, edgeTasks, routing) cloned from the original pane.
2. If `remakeStation` (Station ObjectId) is provided, the remake pane is placed at that station (`currentStation: <remakeStation>`, `currentStatus: pending`). If omitted, `currentStation` is `null` (queued). The pane is created with `order: null`.
3. The remake pane belongs to the **same request** as the original order (`request` is copied).
4. The remake pane's `remakeOf` field points to the original defective pane.
5. The claim's `remadePane` field is set to the new pane's ID.
6. A `MaterialLog` entry with `actionType: remake` is created.
7. Real-time events are emitted: `pane:updated`, `station:pane_arrived` (to the target station room if `remakeStation` was provided), and a `notification` to the original order's `assignedTo` worker.

The frontend should send `remakeStation` with the ObjectId of the order release station (identified by its `OrderReleasePanel` UI block).

This fires regardless of the `decision` field (both `keep` and `destroy`). Setting `status` to `rejected` does **not** create a remake pane. Re-approving an already-approved claim (or one that already has a `remadePane`) does **not** create a duplicate.
Requires `notifications:view` permission. Users without `notifications:manage` permission can only update their own notifications.

get.description: Returns panes matching optional filters. Does **not** use cursor/offset pagination — returns up to `limit` results (default 200, max 1000) sorted by `sort` (default `-createdAt`).

parameters.name: station
parameters.description: Filter by current station ObjectId. Use the string `null` to find panes in virtual states (queued, completed, claimed).

parameters.name: status_ne
parameters.description: Exclude panes with this status (not-equal filter). Useful for excluding claimed panes from order views: `?order=<id>&status_ne=claimed`. If both `status` and `status_ne` are provided, `status_ne` takes precedence.

parameters.name: laminateRole
parameters.description: Filter by laminate role. Use `parent` to get only parent panes, `sheet` for raw sheets, `single` for non-laminate panes.

parameters.name: parentPane
parameters.description: Filter by parent pane ID. Returns all child sheets belonging to a specific parent pane.

post.description:
Requires `panes:create` permission.

**Laminate auto-splitting:** When `rawGlass.sheetsPerPane` is greater than 1 **and** the `routing` array includes a station with `isLaminateStation: true`, the endpoint automatically creates a laminate pane structure instead of a single pane:
- 1 **parent** pane (`laminateRole: "parent"`) with routing set to post-lamination stations only, `currentStation: null`, `currentStatus: "pending"`
- N **sheet** panes (`laminateRole: "sheet"`) with routing set to pre-lamination stations (up to and including the laminate station), each starting at the first station
- Parent and sheets are linked via `childPanes` / `parentPane`

The response shape changes to `{ parent: Pane, sheets: Pane[] }` instead of a single `Pane`.

Returns `400` if `sheetsPerPane > 1` but no laminate station is found in the routing.

201.description: Pane created. For regular panes, `data` is a single Pane object. For laminate panes (`sheetsPerPane > 1`), `data` is `{ parent: Pane, sheets: Pane[] }`.

get.summary: Get pane by ID or pane number
get.description: Accepts either a MongoDB ObjectId or a pane number (e.g. `PNE-0001`). The endpoint auto-detects the format and queries accordingly.

patch.description:
Requires `panes:manage` permission.

**Laminate auto-splitting:** When a pane with `laminateRole: "single"` is updated with `routing` that includes a laminate station, and `rawGlass.sheetsPerPane` is greater than 1 (either from the update body or from the existing pane data), the endpoint automatically splits the pane into a laminate structure:
- The existing pane becomes the **parent** (`laminateRole: "parent"`) with routing set to post-lamination stations only
- N **sheet** panes are created (`laminateRole: "sheet"`) with routing up to and including the laminate station, each starting at the first station
- Parent and sheets are linked via `childPanes` / `parentPane`
- Response shape becomes `{ parent: Pane, sheets: Pane[] }`

This is the typical flow: panes are created without routing at Order Release, then when routing is assigned during release, the laminate split happens automatically.

**Order release side effect:** When a pane's `order` field is set and the pane was previously unlinked (`order: null`), the target order's `paneCount` is automatically incremented and `progressPercent` is recalculated. This is the mechanism used to release a remake pane into an order — the remake pane is created with `order: null` during claim approval, then later assigned to the order via this endpoint, which keeps the order's pane count accurate. A real-time `order:updated` event is emitted.

200.description: Pane updated. For regular updates, `data` is a single Pane object. When laminate split is triggered, `data` is `{ parent: Pane, sheets: Pane[] }`.

oneOf.description: Laminate pane structure (when split is triggered)

post.description: Core endpoint — scan QR, log action, update pane state.

Core endpoint for the QR scanning workflow. Accepts a pane number (from the QR code) and an action to perform at a given station.

**QR codes** contain values like `STDPLUS:PNE-0001`. The frontend should strip the `STDPLUS:` prefix and send only `PNE-0001` as the `paneNumber` path parameter.

**Actions:**
- `scan_in` — Sets `currentStation` to the station, `currentStatus` to `in_progress`, and `startedAt` (if not already set). For sheet panes at a laminate station, emits `laminate:ready` or `laminate:waiting` WebSocket events.
- `start` — Sets `currentStation` to the station and `currentStatus` to `in_progress`.
- `complete` — Sets `currentStation` to the station and `currentStatus` to `awaiting_scan_out`.
- `scan_out` — Requires status `awaiting_scan_out`. Advances the pane to the next station in its routing with status `pending`. If it's the last station, pane status becomes `completed` and `completedAt` is set. Sheet panes do not update `panesCompleted`/`progressPercent`.
- `laminate` — **Must be called on a parent pane**. Validates all active child sheets (status != claimed) are present at the lamination station. Marks all active child sheets as `completed`, sets the parent pane to `awaiting_scan_out` at the lamination station (does not auto-advance). The worker must then `scan_out` the parent from the lamination station to advance it to the first post-lamination station. Creates `laminate_complete` PaneLogs for children and `laminate_start` for the parent. Updates `order.stationBreakdown`. Returns `mergedSheets` count.

**Side effects (every action):**
- Creates a `PaneLog` entry.
- Emits `pane:updated` with `action: "scanned"` to `dashboard`, `pane`, `production`, `station:<name>`.
- Emits `log:updated` with `action: "pane_scanned"` to `log`.
- If the pane has no `material` but has an `order`, the order's material is backfilled onto the pane.

**Additional side effects (scan_out only):**
- Updates associated `Order` progress (`panesCompleted`, `progressPercent`, `stationBreakdown`) and order `status`.
- Emits `order:updated`, `station:pane_arrived`, and `notification` WebSocket events.
- Creates a `Notification` for the order's assigned worker (if any).
- Emits a station-room `notification` event to `station:<nextStation>` for the pane arrival.

**Validations:**
- `complete` and `scan_out` must be performed at the pane's `currentStation`.
- `scan_out` requires pane status to be `awaiting_scan_out` (i.e. `complete` must be called first).
- Scanning a pane that is already `completed` returns 400.
- `laminate` can only be called on parent panes; all active child sheets must be at the lamination station.

Requires `panes:scan` permission.

parameters.name: paneNumber
parameters.description: The pane number (e.g. `PNE-0001`). Strip the `STDPLUS:` prefix from QR codes before sending.

400.description: Validation error — wrong station, already completed, or invalid operator

post.description:
Performs the same scan action on multiple panes at once within a single transaction. Useful for scanning a group of panes at the same station simultaneously.

**Supported actions:** `scan_in`, `start`, `complete`. The `scan_out` and `laminate` actions are **not** supported in batch mode — use the single-pane scan endpoint for those.

**Behavior:**
- All panes are updated atomically in a MongoDB transaction. If any pane fails validation (e.g. wrong station, already completed), the entire batch is rolled back.
- For `scan_in`/`start`: Sets `currentStation` and `currentStatus` to `in_progress`. Sets `startedAt` if not already set.
- For `complete`: Validates the pane is at the specified station. Sets `currentStatus` to `awaiting_scan_out`.
- Creates a `PaneLog` entry for each pane.
- If a pane has no `material` but has an `order`, the order's material is backfilled.
- After commit, emits `pane:updated` and `log:updated` WebSocket events for each pane.

Requires `panes:scan` permission.

paneNumbers.description: Array of pane numbers to scan (e.g. ["PNE-0001", "PNE-0002"])

station.description: Station ObjectId where the scan is performed

action.description: Action to perform on all panes

400.description: Validation error — unsupported action, empty pane list, or invalid data

500.description: Transaction failed — wrong station, pane already completed, or no valid panes found

get.description:
Returns pane scan/action logs. Supports filtering by `station`, `action`, `orderId`, `paneId`, and `materialId`. Results are sorted by newest first.

Requires authentication. All roles can read.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of logs to return (default 100, max 500)

get.description:
Returns a merged, chronologically-sorted timeline of all events for a given material. Combines:
- **MaterialLog** entries (import, withdraw, claim, cut, remake)
- **PaneLog** entries (scan_in, start, complete at each station)

Each entry has a `logType` field (`material_log` or `pane_log`) to distinguish the source.

Requires authentication. All roles can read.

parameters.name: materialId
parameters.description: The material ID to get the timeline for

200.description: Merged timeline of material and pane logs

get.description: Returns all job types. Supports `?isActive=true` filter.

get.summary: Get a sticker template by ID

get.description: Returns the singleton pricing settings document. Auto-creates with defaults if none exists. Requires `pricing:view` permission.

glassPrices.description: Nested map: glass type → thickness → { pricePerSqFt, grindingRate }. `grindingRate` is an object keyed by grinding type (e.g. `{ rough: 50, polished: 75 }`). Legacy single-number values may still exist in older data.

oneOf.description: Grinding rates by type (e.g. rough, polished)

put.description: Updates the singleton pricing settings. Requires `pricing:manage` permission. Emits `pricing:updated` WebSocket event to the `pricing` room.

glassPrices.description: Nested map: glass type → thickness → { pricePerSqFt, grindingRate }. `grindingRate` can be a single number (legacy) or an object keyed by grinding type (e.g. `{ rough: 50, polished: 75 }`).

oneOf.description: Grinding rates by type (e.g. rough, polished)
